_note: Rashi (zodiac sign) mantra pages. Rashi->ruling planet is [confirmed classical] (BPHS Ch.4). The remedial mantra = the ruling planet's mantra; mapping rashi to that remedy is [modern convention] built on confirmed rulership (NOT a verbatim scriptural prescription — stated on-page). Mantra Devanagari copied from anchors; homoglyph-gated. Lagna shares the same ruler, so Lagna gets ONE explainer page, not 12.

rashis.whatIs: Mesha (Aries) is the first sign of the zodiac, ruled by Mangal (Mars) in classical Vedic astrology (Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra). For those with the Moon or ascendant in Mesha, the traditional remedial practice is the Mangal (Mars) mantra, chanted to strengthen and harmonise the sign's ruling planet. The widely-used form is "Om Bhaumaya Namah" (Vedic) or the tantric bija "Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah." Note: associating a rashi with its ruler's mantra is a traditional convention derived from classical rulership, not a verbatim scriptural prescription.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ भौमाय नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Bhaumaya Namah
mantras.source: Ruling planet Mangal (BPHS rulership of Mesha)
mantras.englishExplanation: The Mars name mantra, ruler of Mesha; chanted to strengthen the sign's lord.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ क्रां क्रीं क्रौं सः भौमाय नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah
mantras.source: Mantra Mahodadhi (Mangal bija)
mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric Mars bija for Mesha natives, best under a guru.

benefits: Strengthens the sign-lord Mars
Traditionally for courage, drive, and Mangal-related concerns

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra open; bija under a guru.

faqs.question: Which mantra is for Mesha (Aries) rashi?
faqs.answer: Mesha is ruled by Mars, so the Mars mantra is used: "Om Bhaumaya Namah", or the bija "Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah". This follows the planet that rules the sign.

faqs.question: Which planet rules Aries?
faqs.answer: Mars (Mangal) rules Aries (Mesha) in classical Vedic astrology per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

rashis.whatIs: Vrishabha (Taurus) is the second sign of the zodiac, ruled by Shukra (Venus) in classical Vedic astrology. For those with the Moon or ascendant in Vrishabha, the traditional remedial practice is the Shukra (Venus) mantra to strengthen the sign's ruling planet — "Om Shukraya Namah" (Vedic) or the bija "Om Draam Dreem Draum Sah Shukraya Namah." Associating a rashi with its ruler's mantra is a traditional convention derived from classical rulership, not a verbatim scriptural prescription.

faqs.question: Which mantra is for Vrishabha (Taurus) rashi?
faqs.answer: Vrishabha is ruled by Venus, so the Venus mantra is used: "Om Shukraya Namah", or the bija "Om Draam Dreem Draum Sah Shukraya Namah".

faqs.question: Which planet rules Taurus?
